# services.postfix

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.postfix`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.postfix.aliasFiles` | `attribute set of absolute path` | Aliases' tables to be compiled and placed into /var/lib/postfix/conf. |
| `services.postfix.aliasMapType` | `one of "hash", "regexp", "pcre"` | The format the alias map should have. Use regexp if you want to use regular expressions. |
| `services.postfix.canonical` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Entries for the {manpage}`canonical(5)` table. |
| `services.postfix.dnsBlacklistOverrides` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | contents of check_client_access for overriding dnsBlacklists |
| `services.postfix.dnsBlacklists` | `list of string` | dns blacklist servers to use with smtpd_client_restrictions |
| `services.postfix.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to run the Postfix mail server. |
| `services.postfix.enableHeaderChecks` | `boolean` | Whether to enable postfix header checks |
| `services.postfix.enableSmtp` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the `smtp` service configured in the master.cf. This service listens for plain text SMTP connections on port 25 and supports explicit TLS via StartTLS. It is the primary port used by SMTP servers to exchange mail. |
| `services.postfix.enableSubmission` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the `submission` service configured in master.cf. This service listens for plain text SMTP connections on port 587 and supports explicit TLS via StartTLS. It is a way for clients to login and submit mails after an inband connection upgrade using StartTLS. ::: {.warning} [RFC 8314](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8314) discourages the use of explicit TLS for mail submissionn. ::: |
| `services.postfix.enableSubmissions` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the `submissions` service configured in master.cf. This service listen for implicit TLS connections on port 465. ::: {.info} Per [RFC 8314](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8314) implicit TLS is recommended for mail submission. ::: |
| `services.postfix.extraAliases` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Additional entries to put verbatim into aliases file, cf. man-page {manpage}`aliases(8)`. |
| `services.postfix.extraHeaderChecks` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra lines to /etc/postfix/header_checks file. |
| `services.postfix.extraMasterConf` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra lines to append to the generated master.cf file. |
| `services.postfix.group` | `string` | What to call the Postfix group (must be used only for postfix). |
| `services.postfix.headerChecks` | `list of (submodule)` | Postfix header checks. |
| `services.postfix.headerChecks.*.action` | `string` | The action to be executed when the pattern is matched |
| `services.postfix.headerChecks.*.pattern` | `string` | A regexp pattern matching the header |
| `services.postfix.localRecipients` | `null or (list of string)` | List of accepted local users. Specify a bare username, an `"@domain.tld"` wild-card, or a complete `"user@domain.tld"` address. If set, these names end up in the local recipient map -- see the {manpage}`local(8)` man-page -- and effectively replace the system user database lookup that's otherwise used by default. |
| `services.postfix.mapFiles` | `attribute set of absolute path` | Maps to be compiled and placed into /var/lib/postfix/conf. |
| `services.postfix.package` | `package` | The postfix package to use. |
| `services.postfix.postmasterAlias` | `string` | Who should receive postmaster e-mail. Multiple values can be added by separating values with comma. |
| `services.postfix.rootAlias` | `string` | Who should receive root e-mail. Blank for no redirection. Multiple values can be added by separating values with comma. |
| `services.postfix.setSendmail` | `boolean` | Whether to set the system sendmail to postfix's. |
| `services.postfix.setgidGroup` | `string` | How to call postfix setgid group (for postdrop). Should be uniquely used group. |
| `services.postfix.settings.main` | `open submodule of attribute set of (null or boolean or signed integer or absolute path or string or list of string)` | The main.cf configuration file as key value set. Null values will not be rendered. ::: {.tip} Check `postconf -d` for the default values of all settings. ::: |
| `services.postfix.settings.main.message_size_limit` | `null or signed integer` | Maximum size of an email message in bytes. <https://www.postfix.org/postconf.5.html#message_size_limit> |
| `services.postfix.settings.main.mydestination` | `null or string or list of string` | List of domain names intended for local delivery using /etc/passwd and /etc/aliases. ::: {.warning} Do not include [virtual](https://www.postfix.org/VIRTUAL_README.html) domains in this list. ::: <https://www.postfix.org/postconf.5.html#mydestination> |
| `services.postfix.settings.main.myhostname` | `null or string` | The internet hostname of this mail system. Leave unset to default to the system hostname with the {option}`mydomain` suffix. <https://www.postfix.org/postconf.5.html#myhostname> |
| `services.postfix.settings.main.mynetworks` | `null or (list of string)` | List of trusted remote SMTP clients, that are allowed to relay mail. Leave unset to let Postfix populate this list based on the {option}`mynetworks_style` setting. <https://www.postfix.org/postconf.5.html#mynetworks> |
| `services.postfix.settings.main.mynetworks_style` | `null or one of "host", "subnet", "class"` | The method used for generating the default value for {option}`mynetworks`, if that option is unset. <https://www.postfix.org/postconf.5.html#mynetworks_style> |
| `services.postfix.settings.main.recipient_delimiter` | `null or string` | Set of characters used as the delimiters for address extensions. This allows creating different forwarding rules per extension. <https://www.postfix.org/postconf.5.html#recipient_delimiter> |
| `services.postfix.settings.main.relay_domains` | `null or (list of string)` | List of domains delivered via the relay transport. <https://www.postfix.org/postconf.5.html#relay_domains> |
| `services.postfix.settings.main.relayhost` | `null or (list of string)` | List of hosts to use for relaying outbound mail. ::: {.note} Putting the hostname in angled brackets, e.g. `[relay.example.com]`, turns off MX and SRV lookups for the hostname. ::: <https://www.postfix.org/postconf.5.html#relayhost> |
| `services.postfix.settings.main.smtp_tls_CAfile` | `absolute path` | File containing CA certificates of root CAs trusted to sign either remote SMTP server certificates or intermediate CA certificates. Defaults to the system CA bundle that is managed through the `security.pki` options. <https://www.postfix.org/postconf.5.html#smtp_tls_CAfile> |
| `services.postfix.settings.main.smtp_tls_security_level` | `one of "none", "may", "encrypt", "dane", "dane-only", "fingerprint", "verify", "secure"` | The client TLS security level. ::: {.tip} Use `dane` with a local DNSSEC validating DNS resolver enabled. ::: <https://www.postfix.org/postconf.5.html#smtp_tls_security_level> |
| `services.postfix.settings.main.smtpd_tls_chain_files` | `list of absolute path` | List of paths to the server private keys and certificates. ::: {.caution} The order of items matters and a private key must always be followed by the corresponding certificate. ::: <https://www.postfix.org/postconf.5.html#smtpd_tls_chain_files> |
| `services.postfix.settings.main.smtpd_tls_security_level` | `one of "none", "may", "encrypt"` | The server TLS security level. Enable TLS by configuring at least `may`. <https://www.postfix.org/postconf.5.html#smtpd_tls_security_level> |
| `services.postfix.settings.master` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | The {file}`master.cf` configuration file as an attribute set of service defitions ::: {.tip} Check <https://www.postfix.org/master.5.html> for possible settings. ::: |
| `services.postfix.settings.master.<name>.args` | `list of string` | Arguments to pass to the {option}`command`. There is no shell processing involved and shell syntax is passed verbatim to the process. |
| `services.postfix.settings.master.<name>.chroot` | `boolean` | Whether the service is chrooted to have only access to the {option}`services.postfix.queueDir` and the closure of store paths specified by the {option}`program` option. |
| `services.postfix.settings.master.<name>.command` | `string` | A program name specifying a Postfix service/daemon process. By default it's the attribute {option}`name`. |
| `services.postfix.settings.master.<name>.maxproc` | `signed integer` | The maximum number of processes to spawn for this service. If the value is `0` it doesn't have any limit. If `null` is given it uses the postfix default of `100`. |
| `services.postfix.settings.master.<name>.name` | `string` | The name of the service to run. Defaults to the attribute set key. |
| `services.postfix.settings.master.<name>.private` | `boolean` | Whether the service's sockets and storage directory is restricted to be only available via the mail system. If `null` is given it uses the postfix default `true`. |
| `services.postfix.settings.master.<name>.privileged` | `boolean` | |
| `services.postfix.settings.master.<name>.type` | `one of "inet", "unix", "unix-dgram", "fifo", "pass"` | The type of the service |
| `services.postfix.settings.master.<name>.wakeup` | `signed integer` | Automatically wake up the service after the specified number of seconds. If `0` is given, never wake the service up. |
| `services.postfix.settings.master.<name>.wakeupUnusedComponent` | `boolean` | If set to `false` the component will only be woken up if it is used. This is equivalent to postfix' notion of adding a question mark behind the wakeup time in {file}`master.cf` |
| `services.postfix.submissionOptions` | `attribute set of string` | Options for the submission config in master.cf |
| `services.postfix.submissionsOptions` | `attribute set of string` | Options for the submission config via smtps in master.cf. smtpd_tls_security_level will be set to encrypt, if it is missing or has one of the values "may" or "none". smtpd_tls_wrappermode with value "yes" will be added automatically. |
| `services.postfix.transport` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Entries for the transport map, cf. man-page {manpage}`transport(5)`. |
| `services.postfix.user` | `string` | What to call the Postfix user (must be used only for postfix). |
| `services.postfix.virtual` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Entries for the virtual alias map, cf. man-page {manpage}`virtual(5)`. |
| `services.postfix.virtualMapType` | `one of "hash", "regexp", "pcre"` | What type of virtual alias map file to use. Use `"regexp"` for regular expressions. |
